# Set up Azure DevOps scanning

Vault Radar allows you to connect to any project on Azure DevOps Cloud and Azure DevOps Server.

There are two ways to set up scanning for Azure DevOps -

- Import a single Azure DevOps project as a data source.

- Import many Azure DevOps projects as a data source host.

To import a single Azure DevOps project as a data source, click **Settings**, then click **Data Sources**.
![Import single Azure DevOps project as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source.png)

To import many Azure DevOps projects together as a data source host, click **Settings**, then click **Data Source Hosts**.
![Import many Azure DevOps projects as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source-host.png)

Additionally, for either option, you can choose to scan with [**HCP Vault Radar Scan**](#scan-with-hcp-vault-radar-scan) or
with the [**Vault Radar Agent Scan**](#scan-with-vault-radar-agent-scan).

# Connect to Azure DevOps

For either the data source or data source host option, scanning can be set up using **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or using **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

## Prerequisites

- User email
- [Personal access token](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/organizations/accounts/use-personal-access-tokens-to-authenticate?view=azure-devops&tabs=Windows) with the right set of scopes
    - Code > Read
    - Entitlements > Read
    - Graph > Read
    - Identity > Read
    - Member Entitlement Management > Read
    - Notifications > Read, Write, Manage
    - Project and Team > Read
    - Service Connections > Read
    - User Profile > Read
    - Work Items > Read, Write

If only a single project is being imported via **Data Sources**, toggle the scope to have access to the organization or project collection that the project belongs to.

If many projects are being imported via **Data Source Hosts**, toggle the access scope to have access to all accessible organizations or all accessible project collections.

![Azure DevOps token access scope](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-access-scopes.png)

## Scan with HCP Vault Radar Scan

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **Azure DevOps Cloud** or **Azure DevOps Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, different input text fields may appear.

1. If you previously selected **Azure DevOps Server**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your Azure DevOps domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `azure-devops.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources** -
    - Enter project name in the **Enter your Azure DevOps project name** text field.

    - For **Azure DevOps Server**, enter the Azure DevOps project collection in the **Enter your Azure DevOps project collection name** text field, and

![Azure DevOps Server scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-server-hcp-data-source.png)

    - For **Azure DevOps Cloud**, enter the Azure DevOps organization in the **Enter your Azure DevOps organization name** text field.

![Azure DevOps Cloud scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-cloud-hcp-data-source.png)

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your Azure DevOps personal access token** text field.

1. Click **Next**.

## Scan with Vault Radar Agent Scan

1. Make sure that the agent pool has been set up, refer to the [agent pool documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) for more information.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **Azure DevOps Cloud** or **Azure DevOps Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, different input text fields may appear.

1. If you previously selected **Azure DevOps Server**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your Azure DevOps domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `azure-devops.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources** -
    - Enter project name in the **Enter your Azure DevOps project name** text field.

    - For **Azure DevOps Server**, enter the Azure DevOps project collection in the **Enter your Azure DevOps project collection name** text field, and

![Azure DevOps Server scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-server-agent-data-source.png)

    - For **Azure DevOps Cloud**, enter the Azure DevOps organization in the **Enter your Azure DevOps organization name** text field.

![Azure DevOps Cloud scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-cloud-agent-data-source.png)

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment. This should be in the format `token_creator_email:api_token`.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the name of the environment variable in the **Enter environment path to your Azure DevOps personal access token** text field with the `env://` prefix.

    **Example:**

    ```plaintext
    env://AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN
    ```

1. Click **Next**.

# Finish data source import

1. Choose which repositories to monitor by selecting either the **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**

    Monitor active repositories up to limit ![Add Azure DevOps all active](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-monitor-all-active-resources.png)

    Monitor selected repositories ![Add Azure DevOps custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-monitor-custom-select-resources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

# Finish data source host import

1. Choose which data sources to monitor by selecting either the **All data sources** or **Select data sources**

    Monitor all data sources up to limit ![Add Azure DevOps all](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-monitor-all-active-data-sources.png)

    Monitor selected data sources ![Add Azure DevOps custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-monitor-custom-select-data-sources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

# Update data source

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Sources**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored resources](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-update-data-source.png)

1. To update the monitored repositories, click **Edit monitored resources**.

1. If you imported the **Data Source** directly, to update the token, click **Edit data source details**.

1. If these data sources were discovered by importing the **Data Source Host**, to update the token, [update the data source host instead](#update-data-source-host)

# Update data source host

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Source Hosts**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored data sources](/img/docs/vault-radar/azure-devops/azure-devops-update-data-source-host.png)

1. To update the monitored data sources, click **Edit data sources**

1. To update the token, click **Edit data source host details**
